inputs.keywords: WIMP detection, annual modulation signal, DAMA/LIBRA, xenon detectors, background radiation, seasonal variation, unexplained signals, null results paradox
inputs.additional_information: Anomaly context: DAMA/LIBRA experiment has consistently observed annual modulation signal for 20+ years, claiming dark matter detection. However, xenon-based experiments (XENON1T, LUX, PandaX) with higher sensitivity report null results, contradicting DAMA claims. Additional anomalies: Unexpected electron recoil excess in XENON1T, anomalous gamma-ray excess from galactic center, discrepancy between direct detection limits and astrophysical observations. Context: Standard WIMP paradigm increasingly challenged, yet alternatives (axions, sterile neutrinos) face own unexplained observations. Generate 1-2 disruptive hypotheses that could reconcile these contradictions by challenging fundamental assumptions about dark matter properties, detection methods, or theoretical framework.
